creator: Lauren Forristal
title: (No AI) How to use Google’s new AI agents to go beyond your standard searches

content: Google is launching AI-powered “information agents” that can monitor topics in the background and proactively alert users to updates and changes.

title: (No AI) Google’s AI future demands trust — and your personal data
author: Emma Roth
content:
![A Gemini Spark graphic](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/05/gemini-spark-tasks.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Google has big promises for its AI-powered future - and a lot of it depends on your trust. At I/O 2026, Google described a bunch of new tools that it claims will make your life easier. [Gemini Spark](https://www.theverge.com/tech/932996/google-gemini-spark-antigravity-io-2026), Google's always-on AI agent, can help organize an upcoming event, while Daily Brief can offer a rundown of what to expect during your day. Google is even expanding access to [Gmail's AI inbox](https://www.theverge.com/tech/932973/google-gmail-live-ai-keep-docs-io-2026), which can generate custom to-do lists and draft personalized replies based on your emails.

Many of these features seem genuinely useful, but at the heart of each of them is an AI engine that runs on a trove of personal information. While other AI companies, …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/934172/google-io-gemini-ai-trust-personal-data)
contentSnippet:
Google has big promises for its AI-powered future - and a lot of it depends on your trust. At I/O 2026, Google described a bunch of new tools that it claims will make your life easier. Gemini Spark, Google's always-on AI agent, can help organize an upcoming event, while Daily Brief can offer a rundown of what to expect during your day. Google is even expanding access to Gmail's AI inbox, which can generate custom to-do lists and draft personalized replies based on your emails.
Many of these features seem genuinely useful, but at the heart of each of them is an AI engine that runs on a trove of personal information. While other AI companies, …
Read the full story at The Verge.

Gemini Spark, Google's always-on AI agent, can help organize an upcoming event, while Daily Brief can offer a rundown […]

title: (No AI) Gemini will use Volvo’s external cameras to interpret parking signs
author: Andrew J. Hawkins
content:
![Volvo EX60](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/05/Volvo-EX60-Oculus-6.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Gemini is gaining the power of sight _and_ mobility. [Today at the I/O conference](https://www.theverge.com/tech/932454/google-io-2026-news-announcements), Google and Volvo announced that the AI-powered assistant will be able to access external cameras in [the upcoming EX60 SUV](https://www.theverge.com/news/864561/volvo-ex60-range-price-specs) to help explain and interpret its surroundings to vehicle owners. The upgrade is possible thanks to Volvo's use of Google's embedded Android Automotive as its vehicle operating system.

Google posits that the first use case will be to ask Gemini to translate difficult-to-understand parking signs, though the company obviously sees other future applications as possible as well. Google envisions a camera-enabled Gemini recalling a road sign, inte …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/transportation/933556/google-io-gemini-volvo-ex60-camera-ai-parking)

title: (No AI) Advancing content provenance for a safer, more transparent AI ecosystem

contentSnippet: OpenAI advances AI content provenance with Content Credentials, SynthID, and a verification tool to help people identify and trust AI-generated media.

creator: Boone Ashworth, Michael Calore
title: (No AI) Everything Announced at Google I/O 2026: Gemini, Search, Smart Glasses

content: Google is sprucing up its Gemini models, revamping search, and enabling AI agents in everything. There are also some spiffy new smart glasses coming this fall.

creator: Paresh Dave, Lauren Goode, Zoë Schiffer
title: (No AI) Meta Employees Are Scrambling to Use Up Benefits Ahead of Layoffs
dc:creator: Paresh Dave, Lauren Goode, Zoë Schiffer
content: On the eve of about 8,000 jobs being cut, employees are cashing in on headphone stipends and other perks while they still can.
contentSnippet: On the eve of about 8,000 jobs being cut, employees are cashing in on headphone stipends and other perks while they still can.
